tool.name: Taffy coverage
tool.description: computes basic pairwise coverage stats for a TAF file
tool.command:
## Set variables @SET_UNCOMPRESS_NAMES@
## Uncompress input files if necessary and create symlinks for uncompressed files @UNCOMPRESS_TAF_AND_MAF@
## Run main command taffy coverage --logLevel 'INFO' --inputFile '$uncompressed_input' #if $reference.type == 'specific': --reference '$reference.reference' #end if #if $genomeNames.parsing == 'specific': --genomeNames '$genomeNames.genomeNames' #end if #if $gapThreshold.type == 'specific': #for $threshold in $str($gapThreshold.gapThreshold).split(): #if $threshold.isdigit(): --gapThreshold '$threshold' #end if #end for #end if #if $sexChr.type == 'specific': #for $chr in $str($sexChr.sexChr).split(): --sexChr '$chr' #end for #end if > '$out_file'

param.name: type
param.label: Reference genome
param.help: Coverage is calculated relative to the sequence in the first row of each alignment block. Specify a reference genome to force a specific genome to be the reference, ignoring blocks where it is not present
param.option: Use first row of each alignment block (default)

param.name: parsing
param.label: Genome name parsing
param.help: Genome names are auto-detected by taking the prefix before the first dot (e.g. 'hg38.chr1' -> 'hg38'). All sequences with the same genome name are pooled for coverage calculation. If your genome names contain dots (e.g. 'E.coli'), provide a genome list to prevent incorrect parsing specifically for them

param.name: genomeNames
param.label: Genome name list
param.help: Space-separated list of genome names (e.g. 'E.coli C.elegans S.aureus.USA300')

param.name: type
param.label: Gap handling
param.help: Coverage is calculated based on the total length of the reference contigs. Specify gap size thresholds to exclude gaps larger than a specific size (e.g. unsequenced regions) from the reference length calculation

param.name: gapThreshold
param.label: Gap size thresholds
param.help: Space-separated list of gap sizes in base pairs (e.g. '10000 50000'). Coverage statistics will be calculated by excluding gaps larger than these thresholds. Each provided threshold will result in a separate row in the output

validator.message: Provide a space-separated list of positive numbers

param.name: type
param.label: Sex chromosome handling
param.help: Statistics are aggregated for the whole reference genome. Specify sex chromosomes to separate statistics for autosomes and sex chromosomes
param.option: No separation, treating all as one set (default)

param.name: sexChr
param.label: Sex chromosome contigs
param.help: Space-separated list of full reference contig names (e.g. 'hg38.chrX hg38.chrY'). Output will include specific statistics for autosomes vs. sex chromosomes.

tool.help:
Taffy coverage calculates basic pairwise coverage statistics between a reference genome and other genomes in a TAF file. Coverage is reported both as the number of aligned base pairs and as a fraction of the reference sequence. This tool is useful for evaluating how much of a reference genome is aligned to other genomes and for comparing alignment completeness across species. The output is a tabular file with the following columns:
- **contig**: The name of the reference contig. - **max-gap**: The gap filter used. Reference regions located in a gap larger than this value are excluded from the length (len). - **len**: The effective length of the reference in base pairs (bp). - **query**: The name of the genome being compared against the reference. - **aln**: Coverage: The fraction of the reference (len) that is aligned in the query genome. Formula: aln-bp / len. - **ident**: Identity: The fraction of aligned bases that are exactly identical (measure of similarity in present regions). Formula: ident-bp / aln-bp. - **1:1-aln**: Single-Copy Coverage: Like aln, but only counts regions where both reference and query appear exactly once in the alignment block (no duplications/paralogs). - **1:1-ident**: Single-Copy Identity: Like ident, but only for 1:1 regions. - **aln-bp**: The absolute number of bases that are aligned (no gap, and no 'N'). - **ident-bp**: The absolute number of bases that are aligned and identical. - **1:1-aln-bp**: Absolute number of aligned bases in 1:1 regions. - **1:1-ident-bp**: Absolute number of identical bases in 1:1 regions.
